summary: PR 2511 is a focused tylist decomp/matching update. It matches five tylist functions, improves several remaining tylist items including a new partial decomp of un_80313774, moves shared ToyGlobals/ToySubStruct typing into ty/types.h, refines TyListArg/TyListState fields, and updates un_8031305C's prototype. The bot report records +2552 matched-code bytes and one .data regression tied to this slice.

key_files.role: Main decomp/matching work for tylist functions, including un_803124BC, un_8031263C, un_8031305C, un_80313508, un_80313774, fn_8031438C, and un_803147C4.
Recovered tylist/toy shared struct fields: TyListArg links/jobjs/texts arrays, TyListState x2C2, and unified ToyGlobalsS_/ToySubStructS_ definitions.
Header prototype adjusted for un_8031305C return type from s8 to s32.
Removed duplicate local toy struct definitions and switched casts/usages to the shared ToyGlobalsS_ type.

what_changed: Matched-code report shows five new 100% tylist matches: un_8031263C, fn_8031438C, un_8031305C, un_80313508, and un_803124BC.
un_80313774 changed from a placeholder comment to a substantial partial implementation, with bot-reported score improvement from 0.00% to 62.48%.
TyListArg fields were consolidated from individually named offset fields into role-based arrays: links[3], jobjs[3], and texts[3].
TyListState gained explicit x2C2 and adjusted padding around 0x2B9-0x2C3.
ToyGlobalsS_ and ToySubStructS_ moved into ty/types.h and toy.c now uses the shared ToyGlobalsS_ instead of several narrow local overlay structs.
un_80313508 replaced expanded JObj translate/dirty/assert blocks with HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z helper calls and clearer parent/non-parent handling.
un_8031305C return type changed from s8 to s32 in both tylist.c and tylist.h; its PAD_STACK changed from 8 to 0xC.
Some archive symbol string literals in un_803124BC and un_8031263C were replaced with offsets from un_803FDD18, while the bot report notes a .data regression from 20.07% to 4.40%.
fn_8031438C replaced raw archive/state pointer arithmetic with recovered fields such as archive->jobjs[i], state->x2C2, and a temporary TyListWaitData view.
smart_moves: Moved duplicated ToyGlobals/ToySubStruct overlays out of toy.c into a shared header so both toy.c and tylist.c can use one wider recovered struct instead of several offset-specific local structs.
Used role-based arrays for repeated TyListArg slots, making linked-list navigation and JObj/text slots clearer without over-naming every element.
Mapped generated JObj translate/assert code back to HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z helpers, aligning with the loaded header-inlines standard.
Recovered natural loops for entry-link initialization and list-entry setup in un_80313774 instead of leaving a placeholder or repeated generated-looking blocks.
Kept the prototype surface consistent by updating tylist.h when un_8031305C's body changed return type.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When several local overlay structs represent different known offsets of the same global object, a single wider shared struct can reduce casts and make cross-file use more truthful.
Repeated sibling fields in structs can often be recovered as arrays when code indexes or walks them in regular roles, as shown by TyListArg links/jobjs/texts.
Expanded JObj translate assignments with generated asserts can be replaced by canonical HSD_JObjSetTranslate* helpers when the operation is exactly setting translate components.
Function signature recovery should update both the source definition and the owning header; this PR changed un_8031305C from s8 to s32 in both places.

naming_conventions.name: TyListArg.links
naming_conventions.convention: Use links[0] and links[1] for previous/next-style TyListArg traversal instead of x0 and x4 when the list relationship is evident.

naming_conventions.name: TyListArg.jobjs
naming_conventions.convention: Use jobjs[n] for repeated HSD_JObj slots in TyListArg instead of xC/x10/x14.

naming_conventions.name: TyListArg.texts
naming_conventions.convention: Use texts[n] for repeated HSD_Text slots in TyListArg.

naming_conventions.name: wait_data
naming_conventions.convention: Use a temporary typed view such as TyListWaitData* wait_data when an entry payload has known fields at offsets 0x20 and 0x24.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: un_8031305C changed PAD_STACK from 8 to 0xC, and fn_8031438C keeps PAD_STACK(0x18). The bot report supports these in a merged matching slice, but the PR does not include human explanation for the stack padding.
fn_8031438C adds HSD_Text* new_var and assigns it from state->x290 before writing font_size.y, likely to affect code shape; evidence is the diff only.

un_803124BC and un_8031263C replace inline archive/symbol strings with un_803FDD18-relative offsets. The bot report shows function matches but also a .data regression, so this tactic is evidence-backed for text progress but risky for data matching.
un_80313508 replaced direct translate writes and expanded assert/dirty logic with HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z helper calls.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Typed field access over raw pointer arithmetic
assembly_or_matching_tactics.details: fn_8031438C changes archive offset pointer arithmetic to archive->jobjs[i] and state byte access to state->x2C2; some code in un_803147C4 still uses char* data plus offsets for matching.

review_feedback.feedback: Automated report for GALE01 c14d251..0bdec7e shows matched code 67.24%, +0.07%, +2552 bytes, with five new matches and four unmatched-item improvements.
Automated report flags one unmatched-item regression: main/melee/ty/tylist .data decreased by 63 bytes, from 20.07% to 4.40%.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: PR 2511 tylist .data regression un_803FDD18 string literal offsets
TyListArg links jobjs texts field recovery
ToyGlobalsS_ shared struct ty types.h toy.c

notes: PR body is empty and there are no loaded human review comments, so author intent and reviewer rationale are not available.
The automated bot report provides concrete matching evidence for changed functions and records one data regression.
The raw diff available in the loaded prompt is truncated, but targeted local diff lookup covered the tylist.h and types.h hunks.
No build logs beyond the bot match report are present in the slice.
The string-literal-to-data-offset edits are visible in the diff and correlated with a bot-reported .data regression, but the slice does not prove the author's intended data-ownership rationale.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2511 typed TyListArg repeated slots as arrays
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2511, TyListArg x0/x4/xC/x10/x14-style fields were refactored into links[3], jobjs[3], and texts[3], and tylist.c callsites were updated accordingly. This is a PR-specific example of replacing offset fields with typed repeated slots when the code demonstrates list links and repeated object/text roles.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2511 mapped expanded JObj translate code to helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: In un_80313508, expanded direct translate writes and assert/dirty blocks were replaced with HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z calls, producing a bot-reported 100% match for the function.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2511 shared ToyGlobalsS_ across ty files
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2511 removed several narrow local toy.c overlay structs and added a wider ToyGlobalsS_ plus ToySubStructS_ to ty/types.h, with toy.c switching casts to the shared type.

source_update_candidates.title: Track tylist data-offset string references from PR 2511
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2511 replaced several tylist archive/symbol string literals with offsets from un_803FDD18, including TyDatai.dat/TyDatai.usd and ty model table labels. The bot report shows text-section function matches but also a .data regression. Curator should decide whether these offsets represent real data ownership facts or matching-only pressure before any data-sheet update.
source_update_candidates.reason: The edit touches data/literal ownership and produced a recorded .data regression; it should be reviewed as possible data-sheet/path evidence, not promoted from this intake.

source_update_candidates.title: ty/types.h owns shared ty ToyGlobalsS_ after PR 2511
source_update_candidates.text: The PR moved ToyGlobalsS_ and ToySubStructS_ definitions from toy.c-local declarations into src/melee/ty/types.h and updated toy.c casts to use the shared ToyGlobalsS_. Curator may consider recording ty/types.h as the shared type home for these ty/toy structures if corroborated by current source.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a source-layout/type-ownership fact candidate and should be confirmed against current source before recording.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that string literals should generally be converted to data+offset expressions; this PR's bot report includes a .data regression and the loaded standards warn against string-literal symbol regressions without ownership evidence.
Do not treat PAD_STACK(0xC), the new_var local, or char* data offset usage as general style; they are narrow matching tactics in this PR and lack explanatory review evidence.
Do not infer semantic meanings for remaining xNN fields such as x2A1, x29F, x2B8, or x2C2 beyond the field names shown in the diff.
